message:
fcp-host/enforcement: BudgetCheck allows usage exactly at the limit, matching BudgetTracker semantics

`EnforcementCheck::BudgetCheck` was using `used < limit` for its
Allow outcome, so a request that brought `used` exactly equal to the
advertised budget limit was classified as
`Deny { reason_code: "BUDGET_EXCEEDED" }`. That disagrees with
`budget::BudgetTracker::record_usage`, which classifies the same
exact-equality case as `BudgetAction::Allow` on the basis that the
request is the one that closes the budget and subsequent requests
are the ones that get denied.

The two subsystems need to agree: callers quote `BudgetTracker` in
their internal accounting and then pass the resulting `used` /
`limit` into `BudgetCheck` as a trust check. If one says Allow and
the other says Deny at exact-equality, a tracker-allowed usage event
gets rolled back at the enforcement layer.

Changes in crates/fcp-host/src/enforcement.rs:

* `BudgetCheck::check`: change the Allow guard from
  `(Some(used), Some(limit)) if used < limit` to `... if used <=
  limit`. The following `(Some(used), Some(limit)) =>
  CheckOutcome::Deny` arm still handles `used > limit`, unchanged,
  and its "budget used {used} has reached limit {limit}" explanation
  remains accurate for the strictly-over case.
* Rename the existing test `budget_deny_at_limit` to
  `budget_allow_at_limit` and flip its assertion to
  `outcome.is_allow()`. This captures the new semantics at the
  exact boundary.
* Update `budget_deny_explanation_includes_numbers`: bump
  `ctx.budget_used` from 50 to 51 so the test still exercises a
  Deny outcome (now that 50/50 is Allow). The explanation-format
  assertion is unchanged.
* Add new cross-module regression test
  `budget_check_matches_budget_tracker_at_limit_semantics`:
  - Builds a `fcp_kernel::UsageBudgetPolicy` with a 100-token,
    60-second window and `BudgetEnforcement::Deny`.
  - Feeds `record_usage(&zone, &policy, &[UsageMetric::tokens(100)])`
    into a fresh `BudgetTracker` and asserts the returned eval
    reports `BudgetAction::Allow` at exact equality.
  - Copies `eval.snapshot.budgets[0].used` / `.limit` into a fresh
    enforcement `Context` and runs `BudgetCheck.check(...)` against
    a default `EnforcementConfig`, asserting Allow with an explicit
    message "budget enforcement must agree with budget tracker
    exact-limit semantics". This makes any future regression of
    either side visible from a single test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
